title: colubrina glandulosa subsp. antillana
intro: Colubrina glandulosa subsp. antillana, also known as the Antillean Colubrina or Antillean Colubrina bush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rhamnaceae, native to the Caribbean region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's leaves and bark are used in herbal preparations, often in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, to harness its health benefits, which include reducing pain and inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been studied for their potential antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, further solidifying its importance in traditional medicine.
